programme.desc: Film experts reflect on the life and career of Oscar-winning actor Walter Matthau, who starred in the comedies Fortune Cookie, The Odd Couple and Grumpy Old Men.

programme.title: Discovering: Barbara Stanwyck
programme.desc: A profile of the American actress, who enjoyed a 60-year career in film and TV and best known for her strong roles in films such as Double Indemnity and Stella Dallas.

programme.title: Discovering: Gregory Peck
programme.desc: A profile of the hugely popular American actor who was nominated for five Academy Awards, winning for his role as Atticus Finch in 1962 film To Kill a Mockingbird.

programme.title: The Directors
programme.desc: A look at the life and career of American director David Lynch, whose films include Eraserhead, Mulholland Drive, Wild at Heart, Blue Velvet and The Elephant Man.

programme.title: The Directors
programme.desc: The life and career of screenwriter and director Preston Sturges, whose films include Sullivan's Travels and The Great McGinty, for which he won an Oscar for original screenplay.

programme.title: The Man Who Bought Cricket
programme.desc: The story of Allen Stanford's plan to revolutionise the game of cricket, which fell apart when he was exposed as a financial fraudster.

programme.title: The Man Who Bought Cricket
programme.desc: Now bankrupt, Allen Stanford spies a new opportunity in Montserrat. However, his new venture attracts the attention of US and UK law enforcement agencies.

programme.title: The Man Who Bought Cricket
programme.desc: Allen Stanford moves his operation to Antigua where he discovers the power of cricket in the Caribbean.

programme.title: Dogtown and Z-Boys
programme.desc: Documentary charting the history of skateboarding, from its 1970s emergence as a land-based form of surfing in Los Angeles and its subsequent explosion of popularity worldwide, to its current status as a widely-practised extreme sport. Narrated by Sean Penn, with contributions from experts Jay Adams and Tony Alva.

programme.title: My Icon: Thierry Henry
programme.desc: The former Arsenal and France forward speaks about the individuals that have been an inspiration to him over the course of his career.

programme.title: Bardot
programme.desc: A look at the life and career of actress Brigitte Bardot, examining the hardships, trials and struggles she faced as well as her work and legacy.

programme.title: I Am Burt Reynolds
programme.desc: Family and friends of the iconic Hollywood actor come together to share memories of his life and fruitful career, from its beginnings in Old Hollywood through to breakout roles in Deliverance and The Longest Yard, to the smash hit, Smokey and the Bandit and on to Boogie Nights.

programme.title: The Bee Gees: How Can You Mend a Broken Heart
programme.desc: Documentary exploring the history of the group, featuring contributions from surviving member Barry Gibb as well as archive interviews with his brothers Robin and Maurice.

programme.title: The Truth vs Alex Jones
programme.desc: Following the two defamation lawsuits brought by families of Sandy Hook Elementary School shooting victims against Alex Jones and his website InfoWars.

programme.title: Belushi
programme.desc: The life story of comedy star John Belushi, who captured the hearts and funny-bones of audiences worldwide before his tragic and untimely death aged 33.

programme.title: David Attenborough's Conquest of the Skies
programme.desc: The veteran naturalist turns his attention to creatures that dominate the skies today. He begins with the vast bird family, watching a group of vultures as they go scavenging and some peregrine falcons dive bombing for their prey. He then moves on to the floating forests of Ecuador to find out what makes the humble hummingbird tick, before ending his journey at the Gomantong bat caves of Borneo -

programme.title: David Attenborough's Dragons and Damsels
programme.desc: Arguably more beautiful than butterflies, better fliers than hummingbirds and with intriguing and complex behavioural patterns - dragonflies and damselflies are marvels of the insect kingdom. This documentary uses the latest camera technology to show their manoeuvres, mating strategies, territorial behaviour and flight dynamics, revealing them to be the ultimate insect.

programme.title: Flying Monsters with David Attenborough
programme.desc: The broadcaster explores the world of the pterosaur, a flying creature that occupied the skies 200 million years ago. Visiting sites around the world, from the Dorset coast to New Mexico, he explains how the reptiles evolved to defy the forces of gravity, and examines the features of their anatomy that can still be seen in present-day descendents. He also discovers why they became extinct after do

programme.title: David Attenborough's Natural History Museum Alive
programme.desc: The broadcaster invites viewers to join him as he takes an after-hours stroll around one of his favourite places, the Natural History Museum. But this is no ordinary tour as some of the many exhibits spring to life as the veteran naturalist strolls along.

programme.title: David Attenborough's Global Adventure
programme.desc: Documentary capturing some of the greatest filming sequences of David Attenborough's career, using previously unseen footage, from the deepest dives in a submersible on the Great Barrier Reef to watching a million bats take off in a cave in Borneo. Other highlights include life in the frozen tundra of South Georgia, a colony of King Penguins and the world's largest migration of Hammerhead sharks i

programme.title: Micro Monsters with David Attenborough
programme.desc: David Attenborough employs the latest technologies to explore the violence, rivalries and deadly weaponry existing within the world of bugs. This first episode examines the survival tactics of its terrifying residents including killer ants, trap-setting spiders and beetles with the ability to shoot boiling chemicals at their enemies.

programme.title: Micro Monsters with David Attenborough
programme.desc: The broadcaster continues his bug-eyed view of the world of creepy-crawlies, revealing how predators defuse the defences of their prey. Highlights include the cockroach wasp, busy preparing a tasty - and very live - treat for its young, the whirligig beetle, which employs a water-based radar system, and the jumping portia spider, which feeds on other arachnids.

programme.title: Attenborough at 100: Behind the Lens
programme.desc: As David Attenborough turns 100, this intimate film presents new interviews, behind-the-scenes footage and extraordinary clips.

programme.title: David Attenborough's Wild City
programme.desc: With the latest film-making technology, the veteran broadcaster uncovers the unseen natural treasures of Singapore and explores how the has adapted to humans.

programme.title: David Attenborough's Wild City
programme.desc: The veteran broadcaster narrates a day in the animal life of Singapore's urban environments, from a troop of suburban macaques to a family of otters in Marina Bay.

programme.title: David Attenborough's Wild City
programme.desc: Exploring Singapore's islands, which have a host of remarkable creatures, including the tiger tail seahorse and the quick-changing cuttlefish.

programme.title: Great Barrier Reef with David Attenborough
programme.desc: Following his visit to the Great Barrier Reef in 1957, naturalist and broadcaster David Attenborough returns and uses the latest filming techniques to unlock the secrets of the natural wonder. In the first edition, he descends beneath the waves at night and uses cutting-edge technology to generate computer scans of the sea floor. He also learns about a complex community of animals, as well as the

programme.title: Great Barrier Reef with David Attenborough
programme.desc: Using the latest technology, the naturalist explores the shark-infested waters of Osprey Reef in the Triton submersible, before returning to the nesting grounds of the Green Sea Turtle, 60 years after his first visit to Raine Island. programme.title: Great Barrier Reef with David Attenborough
programme.desc: The naturalist examines what the next few decades may hold for this remarkable ecosystem, and looks at the work of scientists who are trying to preserve the area and the species that inhabit it, assisted by research vessel the Alucia. programme.title: David Attenborough Meets Obama
programme.desc: Another chance to see Barack Obama's conversation, recorded in May 2015, with renowned naturalist and film-maker David Attenborough. programme.title: Micro Monsters with David Attenborough
programme.desc: A further selection of creepy-crawly close-ups, focusing on courtship in the bug world. Highlights include scorpions shaking their booty, the perfume-wearing nasonia wasp and the tropical cricket, for whom romance goes out of the window as it uses bribery to seal the deal with would-be partners. One potential partner to steer well clear of, however, is the praying mantis, the female of which is no

programme.title: Micro Monsters with David Attenborough
programme.desc: A close-up view of sex, bug-style, as David Attenborough talks viewers through the different ways in which creepy-crawlies reproduce. Size matters for the minuscule male orb spider, creepily sneaking up on its intended and trying to mate without her noticing, while there's no rest for the lothario-like butterfly, which has plenty of notches on its proverbial bedpost. programme.title: Micro Monsters with David Attenborough
programme.desc: David Attenborough shifts the focus to bugs that prefer co-operation rather than conflict. They include burrowing cockroaches, the suitably named social spiders - which share a 30-metre web - the queen bee and her happy hive of workers and green ants pulling together to build a nest.

programme.title: Micro Monsters with David Attenborough
programme.desc: The broadcaster concludes his close-up examination of the creepy-crawly world, shifting focus to the bugs' ultimate invention - the colony. Key residents of these minuscule mega-cities include honey bees, termites, leafcutter ants - who have a knack for farming - and their Argentine cousins, members of a community so vast it spans an entire continent.

programme.title: David Attenborough's Kingdom of Plants
programme.desc: The naturalist and film-maker explores the many varieties of plant life housed at the Royal Botanic Gardens in Kew, west London, and explains what they reveal about the natural world and the development of life on Earth. programme.title: David Attenborough's Kingdom of Plants

programme.title: David Attenborough's Kingdom of Plants

programme.desc: A group of brothers who inherited a rugged hunting cabin from their father and his friends enlist the team to restore it and preserve its legacy.

programme.desc: A surprise twist sends the crew into overdrive as they battle freezing temps to deliver this dream home; the team bands together to tackle weather setbacks, custom creations, and a pool table install.

programme.title: Maine Cabin Masters

programme.desc: After saving Larry Melton's childhood home, the builders invite him to the Boneyard to help restore his family home; as Larry learns the ropes, Mark arranges a final surprise with some very special guests.

programme.desc: After a bombshell reveal, the finalists perform in front of friends, family and industry leaders who could make their dreams come true.

programme.title: Maine Cabin Masters
programme.desc: If you like your camps old, big, haunted or practically starting from scratch the Maine Cabin Masters have got everyone covered; Dixie and Ryan invite people to be a fifth wheel as they go over the Best of Season 5.

programme.desc: Installing a bathroom recessed cabinet; replacing a chimney that has been causing water damage to an indoor charcoal grill.

programme.title: Ask This Old House
programme.desc: Solving gutter water runoff; lead carpenter Lasana Harper talks about his journey into carpentry; replacing a ceiling fan.

programme.title: This Old House

programme.title: This Old House

programme.title: Storied Spaces
programme.desc: From revitalized estates to a re-conceptualized farmhouse, property owners in Oregon, England, and Italy tackle the caretaking of magnificently sized, grand homes with unique histories and purposes.

programme.title: Storied Spaces
programme.desc: From England to the Dominican Republic and coastal California, adventurous families cultivated their homes with the same goal in mind: achieving a slower pace of life by using a blend of natural materials and indoor-outdoor living.

programme.desc: Chip and Jo take "Fixer Upper" outside of Texas for the first time; getting creative with logistics and design challenges, they begin to transform a 1960s cottage into a retreat for the entire family.

programme.title: Fixer Upper: Colorado Mountain House
programme.desc: Chip and Jo's Colorado fixer heats up fast. Projects stall, plans fall short, and wildlife keeps things interesting; undaunted, they press on to create the mountain retreat they've long dreamed of for their family.
